docs.title: Home
docs.text:
This site presents National Access Point (NAP) Reference Architecture, based on the European ITS Framework Architecture (FRAME) methodology.

NRA aims to harmonize NAP activities at the European level and increase their effectiveness. It defines roles, functions, and processes for NAP users and data registration and data/metadata retrieval. It also outlines the minimum functionality for the two basic NAP types at the national level (Metadata directory and Data Platform).

Main content of this site is a browsable UML model of the NAP Reference Architecture. To complement the NRA model we also include FRAME models for the reference.

    Dive into to model of the NAP Reference Architecture

docs.title: Acknowledgment
docs.text: The NAP Reference Architecture (NRA) was developed within the project FRAME-NEXT and was further upgraded according to the NAP operator needs within the project NAPCORE. Both Programme support actions (PSA) co-financed by EU from Connection European Facility (CEF) programme.

docs.title: The FRAME
docs.text:
FRAME Home Page: https://frame-online.eu

FRAME, an acronym for the FRamework Architecture Made for Europe, is a minimum stable framework necessary for the deployment of integrated and interoperable ITS.

The FRAME is developed as a framework that provides a systematic basis for planning ITS implementations in Europe. It facilitates their integration when deploying multiple systems and helps ensure interoperability, across European countries. A distinctive feature of the FRAME is that it sets out all ITS functionality, from which smaller systems and subsets are created, describing a specific service, or set of services. It offers more than one way of performing a service, allowing users to select the most appropriate set of functionalities for their environment.

The development methodology, supported by computer-based tools, assists in creating logically consistent subsets of the FRAME architecture functional view and subsequent physical, communication and organizational views. Thus, the FRAME architecture serves as a framework from which specific models of integrated ITS can be systematically created.

docs.title: Basic building blocks
docs.text:
For reference purposes we include on this webpage browsable UML models of two basic FRAME building blocks.

  • FRAME Meta model for structure
  • FRAME Repository for content

docs.title: More information and resources
docs.text: More information including downloadable resources, reports, user guides could be found on the website of the FRAME-NEXT project https://frame-next.eu/ in section downloads and documents.

docs.title: NAP Reference Architecture
docs.text:
NAP (National Access Point) Reference Architecture is built using FRAME.

Scope & objectives

The NAP reference architecture describes the minimum required functionalities, processes, and interfaces to enable a harmonized exchange of information (metadata and data) for an EU-wide reuse of data to create the basis for an EU-wide mobility data space.

The NRA describes the minimum functionality for the two basic NAP types the Metadata directory and Data Platform in the following detail:

  • Motivational layer: This answers the question of WHY and sets up the system.
  • Functional view: This describes the bounded functionalities of the system, with information exchange (among functionalities, and with other systems). It also describes pro-cesses realized by the functionalities.
  • Physical view: This describes how the functionality could be implemented in separate sub-systems and identifies the critical interfaces that need to be harmonized.
  • Communications view: This describes the interaction and data exchange between the different components of the system.
  • Organizational (enterprise) view: This describes the relationships between actors in the system.
  • Explore UML model of NAP Reference Architecture including change log
  • Report an issue or download latest NAP versions on GitHub

docs.title: Based on FRAME Repository and FRAME Meta Model
docs.text: The NAP Reference Architecture is using components from FRAME Repository and FRAME Meta Model.

docs.title: Extending standard model where needed
docs.text:
During NAP Reference Architecture development, there were cases when standard FRAME Repository and/or Meta Model did not suffice. In such cases, the standard model was modified.

These modification will eventually be incorporated back into standard FRAME Repository and/or Meta Model in future.

docs.title: FRAME Meta Model
docs.text:
FRAME meta model defines objects used by updated FRAME. The meta-model has a different structuring than the final views of the architecture, it structures the FRAME artefacts into five sections. The first four sections develop from a high-level perspective down to more technical details of the service. The last layer contains artefacts, which are relevant for all sections.

The sections are Motivational Layer, Business Layer, Application Layer, Data Layer and External

docs.title: Browsable HTML UML model
docs.text:
FRAME Meta Model version 5.1.1

Note

The FRAME Meta Model presented here may deviate slightly from the standard to support specific needs of the NAP Reference Architecture, which are not yet supported.

docs.title: Version 5.1.1
docs.text: • Final version of the meta-model from project FRAME-NEXT as available from the project website https://frame-next.eu/ in section downloads and documents.

docs.title: FRAME Repository
docs.text: FRAME repository is a library of User Needs and functions, which are grouped into several Functional Areas covering whole ITS. The repository is used in first phases of creation of the specific architectures, when an architect selects based on the stakeholders aspirations a user needs fulfilling those aspirations and leading to a specific functionality.

docs.title: NAP Reference Architecture
docs.text: The NAP reference architecture is created for two basic NAP types Metadata directory and Data platform and details motivation layer, functional, organizational, physical and communications view as well as a link to NAP KPI framework developed within the NAPCORE project.

docs.text:
NAP Reference Architecture version 0.7.1

Note

The NAP Reference Architecture is always work in progress, it deviates from the FRAME repository since it provides much greater detail.
